classes.reasoning: The document explicitly self-identifies as 'CLINICAL TRIAL PROTOCOL' on the title page and throughout the document (e.g., pages 1-2, headers). It contains comprehensive protocol sections including objectives, study design, methodology, inclusion/exclusion criteria, statistical analysis plan, safety monitoring, and ethical considerations—all characteristic features of a Clinical Study Protocol. The version history on page 2 shows iterative protocol amendments, and the extensive 84-page content covers all expected protocol components.

trial_title: VIRTUOSE: Efficiency of sildenafil on the absolute claudication distance of peripheral arterial disease patients with intermittent claudication. A phase III, National, Multicenter, Prospective, Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-Controlled trial
indication: Peripheral arterial disease with intermittent claudication
summary: This is a Phase III, national, multicenter, prospective, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled clinical trial protocol (VIRTUOSE) evaluating the efficacy of sildenafil 140 mg/day versus placebo on absolute claudication distance in patients with peripheral arterial disease and intermittent claudication. The study is sponsored by CHU de Rennes, with Protocol Version 10.0 dated 26/06/2025, and has undergone multiple amendments (version history shows progression from v1.0 to v10.0). The protocol includes comprehensive sections on study objectives, methodology, patient selection criteria, safety evaluation, statistical analysis, and ethical considerations.
references_to: Protocol versions 1.0 through 10.0 with dates between 29/06/2020 and 26/06/2025
